misconceptionSummary: Students often see division as acting only on the coefficient and do not understand cancellation in the variable part.

visuals.ruleText: A term can be split into a number part and variable factors

steps.narration: Dividing algebraic terms works because a term is built from a coefficient and variable factors.

lines.text: x^3 ÷ x removes one factor of x

steps.narration: Thinking in factors helps. X cubed means x times x times x, so dividing by x removes one of those factors.
Use the same idea on a full term. Six a squared divided by two a becomes three a.
This also appears in context. If you know area and one side of a rectangle, dividing can give the missing side length.

visuals.expression: Division cancels matching factors in the coefficient and variable parts
visuals.label: Think in factors, not just symbols

steps.narration: The main idea is cancellation of matching factors. That is why the powers reduce when dividing algebraic terms.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: dividing the number part only. In algebra, the variable factors are part of the term too, so they must also be simplified.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not stop after dividing the coefficient. The variable factors matter just as much.
